ab: mais isian 2022 painsanan hai namas’ampuk a Sihu mas pali’uni Incumin tu nibunsiu tu nakatasaan a ngaanBunun mapatas
ch: 在2022年，政府召開了原住民身分證可單列族名的會議。
en: In 2022, the government announced that indigenous identification cards can include tribal names separately.

ab: maisingavik mas mabuntu pakasia sihu, nasiza mas nakatasa ngaanBunun mapatas sian nibunsiu tu minduduaz Haisul Palavian.
ch: 我訪問了長期向政府爭取身分證「單列族名」的部落青年Haisul.Palavian。
en: I interviewed Haisul.Palavian, a young tribal member who has long been advocating for the inclusion of tribal names on identification cards.

ab: mapatuzikaangin a saikin mas saicia tu nakausian isaicia tu lumah masingav mas saicia.
ch: 我和他約定好這個假日到他家訪問他。
en: We had arranged to visit his home this holiday for the interview.

ab: masa masisingav hai papasahaalunik tu maikusna’isa a isaicia ngaan. papasahaalku amin mas isaicia tu tastulumah. isaicia tu asang.
ch: 在訪問的過程中，他向我介紹了他的名字的由來，也帶我認識了他的家人、他的部落。
en: During the interview, he introduced to me the origin of his name and also introduced me to his family and tribe.

ab: tupaunku a saia tu: mavia a kasu tu na’asa matsuhis nganbubun i? masial aupa a tatuuna ispatutuki hai?
ch: 我問他為什麼堅持要回復族名，舊的登記方式不好嗎?
en: I asked him why he insisted on changing his name. Wasn’t the old registration method sufficient?

ab: tupaunik mas saicia tu aupa nii a patasantauluu’an mahtu mathas tu mapishaiap mas isaicia tu ngaan. aiza a bunun malala mas saicia tu Hai tais’aan, aat min’uni mas iskangingiit.
ch: 他對我說，因為用中文字並不能準確的傳達他的名字，其他人還會叫他「海先生」，也因此鬧出不少笑話。
en: He told me that using Chinese characters could not accurately convey his name, and others would still call him \"Hai Xian Sheng,\" which resulted in many jokes being made.

ab: tupa a Haisul.Palavian tu: maza maku’uni mas malis’Imcumin mapatas tu ngaan hai imita mamantuk tu ngaan. daihiu a inaak ngaanan mas imita tu siduh, imita tu sinpathaal. hadang madia a tais’aan masuhais mas itu’anak’anak tu ngaan.
ch: Haisul.Palavi說：「原住民語書寫的名字才是我們真正的名字。我的名字代表了我們的族群、我們的家族、我們的身分，希望能有更多族人找回自己的名字。」
en: Noami Kulas said, “The names written in indigenous languages are our true names. My name represents our ethnic group, our family, our identity. I hope more fellow tribespeople can reclaim their own names.”
